layer_1_flower.intent: Citrus-kissed bliss melting into cozy stillness

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Lemongrass carries the dominant myrcene. Lemon matches the yuzu aroma note. Lavender provides Linalool support. Black Pepper rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A low-energy session. Citrus-kissed bliss melting into cozy stillness. Genres: ambient, dub, lo-fi hip hop, deep house. Tempo around 65-90 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Sprite Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Yuzu and propane infused chamomile blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 185°F.
Add chamomile tea bag and steep for 5 minutes.
Add fresh yuzu peel and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add lapsang souchong tea and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Sprite Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz fresh yuzu juice and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz smoked simple syrup.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with yuzu.

cocktail.name: Sprite Sour
method: Combine 2 oz bourbon, 1.5 oz fresh yuzu juice, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz smoked simple syrup.
Dry shake for 5 seconds (if using egg white).
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with yuzu.

juice.name: Sprite Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh yuzu through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Sprite Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz bourbon with 0.75 oz fresh yuzu juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Medjool Dates Stuffed with Goat Cheese
snack.desc: Plump dates filled with herbed chèvre and topped with a walnut half and honey drizzle.

appetizer.name: Wild Mushroom Toast with Thyme
appetizer.desc: Sautéed mixed mushrooms on sourdough with fresh thyme and shaved Parmesan.

main.name: Lamb Tagine with Preserved Lemon
main.desc: Slow-braised lamb with apricots, olives, preserved lemon, and warming spices.

dessert.name: Cardamom Rice Pudding
dessert.desc: Creamy arborio rice pudding with cardamom, rosewater, and crushed pistachios.

cheese.name: Manchego & Membrillo
cheese.desc: Aged Manchego with quince paste, Marcona almonds, and crusty bread.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your citrus-kissed space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Low seating, soft throws, candles at eye level. Minimize overhead lighting.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 30 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the diffuser. Complete all cycles before consumption.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Close your eyes. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, letting the scent fill your lungs. Hold gently for four. Release slowly through the mouth for eight counts, feeling gravity pull you deeper into your seat with each exhale.

layer_13_narrative.text:
23 drops of lemongrass carry the dominant Myrcene through the room slowly — the way Alpenglow Farms intended when they cultivated Sprite in Benbow, Humboldt County. The lineage reads Honey Popz X Pink Runtz, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: yuzu, propane, and tamarind.

The second oil — Lemon — adds 8 drops of brightness that matches the propane note in the flower itself. Blissful is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body settles into cozy with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.58% total can deliver.

In the dimly lit room, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of Myrcene, already primed for citrus-kissed bliss melting into cozy stillness. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Alpenglow Farms grows in Benbow, Humboldt County: not just cannabis, but context. The indica genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as bay laurel on a Humboldt hillside. The session settles into its final form — physically relaxing, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Petite Sirah's dense, jammy character mirrors myrcene's heavy, sedating quality — both deliver weight and body.
Grenache's lush fruit and herbaceous undertones complement myrcene's tropical warmth.

beer.terpene_bridge: Citra and Simcoe hops are myrcene-dominant — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins sharing the same terpene synthesis pathways.
beer.tasting_note: Tropical citrus, pine resin, clean bitter finish

beer.terpene_bridge: EKG hops carry significant myrcene, connecting malt-forward English ales to cannabis terpene profiles.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
